creator: tamelingdaniel@gmail.com
text:
Created attachment 231647
Add ')' and '}' to the word delimiters for the completion. They were missing.

I had a look at what is going on and this looks like a bug. The word delimiters passed to libedit are L" \t\n\"\\'`@$><=;|&{(". This is missing ')' and '}'. So when it is going backwards through
file.\(mine\).
it recognizes the ')' as a normal character, but the '\' is actually a word delimiter and it then thinks that the ')' starts a new word. So when trying to complete the expression it looks for files starting with ")." and if there is, for example, a file ").test", one gets file.\(mine\\).test, which isn't the right thing to do.
What it should do is recognize the ')' as a word delimiter, realize that the '\' before that means that it's escaped and use the whole string for the completion. So one has to add ')' and '}' to the word delimiters. The attached patched fixed the issue in my experiments.
